Preps all lunches for the week on Sunday night — never buys takeout for midday meals
Now books collaborative meetings and brainstorming sessions in the morning with the cross-functional team — prefers meetings earlier in the day to align on project goals
Adds a daily project update note to the cross-functional team workspace before logging off — avoids lengthy recap emails
Reads physical books before bed — avoids all screens, including e-readers and phones, at night during doctor-mandated screen break
Pauses all video game play, including narrative indie games, during mandated screen-free recovery period — instead spends evenings on non-digital pastimes
Spends her usual evening screen time on jigsaw puzzles or sketching in a physical notebook to relax without digital devices
No longer makes dinner for her boyfriend twice a week while he is in Portland; instead, focuses on solo dinners or meals with her dog as companion
Schedules regular video calls with her boyfriend in Portland, prioritizing evenings to stay connected while he's away
Takes longer evening walks with her dog for companionship and stress relief when living alone
Regularly babysits her niece on select weekday evenings after work — no longer restricts babysitting to weekends
Prepares simple batch vegetarian dinners in advance on Sundays to have ready for nights when babysitting her niece after work
Brings calm, screen-free activities (like crocheting or reading aloud) to occupy herself and soothe her niece during weekday babysitting sessions
Now keeps a strict nightly sleep log, tracking bedtime, medication intake, and awakenings as requested by her doctor ahead of her sleep specialist appointment
Only takes lunchtime walks with her dog on remote workdays; eats lunch at her desk or cafeteria when in-office three days a week
Commutes to the office three days a week via light rail — always listens to tech podcasts during transit instead of music or audiobooks
Prepares easy-to-carry vegetarian lunches for office days — avoids meal prep requiring heating or elaborate assembly at work
Calls her parents multiple times a week to coordinate post-surgery support and discuss her mother's recovery; texting becomes secondary during this period
Schedules and prioritizes Tacoma trips around her mother's medical appointments, planning her work and personal activities to minimize stress and maximize support during the recovery period
Brings her dog along on Tacoma visits whenever possible to provide comfort for her mother and herself during lengthy stays
Keeps healthy on-the-go vegetarian snacks and meals ready for long driving days — avoids stopping for fast food or convenience store options while supporting her mother

Checks and responds to emails and notifications in real-time throughout conference days to stay on top of urgent coordination for her workshop and networking events, rather than sticking to her usual set schedule.
Prefers in-person networking during conferences, taking initiative to approach potential partners at workshops and social events instead of waiting for introductions through colleagues.
Chooses vegetarian meal options at conference events and workshops, politely declining meat-based dishes even during formal dinners or receptions.
Documents conference workshop tips and partnership leads in a dedicated travel notebook each evening rather than updating her usual digital workspace on the go.
Switched from crocheting to coloring or simple puzzle books while listening to podcasts — avoids all activities requiring two-handed dexterity until cast is removed
Now relies on dictation software and voice-to-text tools for documenting bugs and writing project notes instead of typing with both hands
Requests help from her boyfriend or orders ready-to-eat vegetarian meals due to difficulty prepping food with one hand
Starts every workday with a cup of herbal tea while reviewing her task list — strictly avoids coffee and caffeinated beverages
Uses her prescribed CPAP machine nightly as her primary sleep intervention — no longer takes sleep medication unless specifically directed by her sleep specialist
Performs a brief calming yoga or gentle stretching routine before bed instead of any strenuous evening activity, as prescribed by her sleep specialist to support better sleep with sleep apnea.
Cleans and checks her CPAP mask nightly as part of her pre-bed routine to prevent discomfort and ensure machine effectiveness.

superseded_preferences.fact: Schedules focus blocks for complex coding tasks in the morning — never books meetings before noon if avoidable
superseded_preferences.reason: Joining the new cross-functional team for the pilot project requires morning collaboration and brainstorming sessions to align on digital innovation strategies, replacing her solitary focus block habit.

superseded_preferences.fact: Completes all bootcamp assignments the same night as class — never lets coursework pile up to the weekend
superseded_preferences.reason: All non-essential screen activities and coursework must pause due to her mandated screen break and work time-off for health reasons; bootcamp sessions and homework are on hold.

superseded_preferences.fact: Takes a brisk 20-minute walk with her dog at lunch daily — never eats lunch at her desk
superseded_preferences.reason: Returning to the office three days per week means she can't walk her dog at lunch unless working remotely, so her habit is now split based on location.

superseded_preferences.fact: Turns off screens 30 minutes before bed and listens to calming music to help fall asleep
superseded_preferences.reason: With the emergence of persistent clinical insomnia and doctor-prescribed medication, her bedtime behavior shifts out of necessity from music/routine-based wind-down to medical intervention as her principal means for falling asleep.

superseded_preferences.fact: Organizes monthly board game nights at her apartment — never rotates hosting to other friends' places
superseded_preferences.reason: She prefers hosting at her apartment, but living alone for three months (with her boyfriend away) makes solo hosting more difficult logistically and emotionally; friends are likely to offer hosting, or gatherings may be postponed, removing this routine.

superseded_preferences.fact: Texts her parents every Sunday evening to check in — avoids long phone calls unless it's a special occasion
superseded_preferences.reason: Her mother's hip replacement surgery and the need for ongoing support and appointment coordination requires more frequent and direct communication than her previous texting-only habit.

superseded_preferences.fact: Babysits her niece on Saturday afternoons — never on weekday evenings
superseded_preferences.reason: Her sister’s new job requires frequent travel, so she agreed to provide recurring weekday babysitting for her infant niece. The timing can no longer be confined to just Saturday afternoons.

superseded_preferences.fact: Makes dinner for her boyfriend twice a week, even if they eat at different times — never orders in on these nights
superseded_preferences.reason: Her boyfriend’s three-month relocation to Portland means he is not home; she cannot cook for him or coordinate dinner routines as before.

superseded_preferences.fact: Initiates group chats for planning friend gatherings — never relies on others to coordinate
superseded_preferences.reason: She will be spending more time supporting her mother in Tacoma and managing frequent drives for medical appointments, making it difficult to reliably initiate friend gathering planning; coordination may shift to friends or pause entirely during this period.

superseded_preferences.fact: Crochets small projects while listening to podcasts — never watches TV at the same time
superseded_preferences.reason: With her fractured wrist in a cast for six weeks, she can't physically crochet small projects, so she replaces this hobby with one-handed creative pastimes like coloring or puzzle books that require less manual dexterity.

superseded_preferences.fact: Explores a new Seattle park or garden every other weekend — avoids crowded indoor attractions
superseded_preferences.reason: Her wrist injury and cast limit mobility and make extended outdoor walks impractical; she cannot safely explore new parks and gardens during recovery.

superseded_preferences.fact: Plays narrative-driven indie games in short evening bursts — never engages in multiplayer online games
superseded_preferences.reason: The doctor ordered a full two-week break from screens; all video gaming is suspended for now to avoid screen exposure.

superseded_preferences.fact: Starts every workday with a hand-brewed cup of coffee while reviewing her task list — never uses a pod machine
superseded_preferences.reason: Her sleep specialist mandated total avoidance of caffeine due to her new sleep apnea diagnosis, so she switched from hand-brewed coffee to caffeine-free tea in the morning.

superseded_preferences.fact: Checks email three times a day on a set schedule — avoids push notifications outside those windows
superseded_preferences.reason: Attending and leading at a national tech conference requires flexible, immediate communication to coordinate workshop logistics and respond to spontaneous networking or partner opportunities, making her rigid scheduled email habit impractical.

superseded_preferences.fact: Walks her dog first thing in the morning, rain or shine — never skips unless ill
superseded_preferences.reason: With new office commute and earlier start times, she is no longer able to consistently walk her dog every morning before work on in-office days. The daily routine is disrupted by the schedule change.

superseded_preferences.fact: Reads on her e-reader for 20 minutes before bed — never uses her phone for reading at night
superseded_preferences.reason: Her doctor has ordered a complete break from screens, including e-readers, for two weeks to address stress-induced health symptoms. She can only read physical books during this period.

superseded_preferences.fact: Sets aside one evening per week to research digital engagement trends and share findings with the team
superseded_preferences.reason: The weekly research activity depends entirely on screen use; with all non-essential work and screens banned for two weeks by her doctor, this routine cannot continue for now.

superseded_preferences.fact: Prefers listening to soothing, non-stimulating music via a speaker or radio instead of streaming playlists from her phone during the recovery period
superseded_preferences.reason: The 'recovery period' mandating this specific music listening setup has ended; her bedtime and relaxation routines are now governed by sleep medication and impending sleep specialist care, making this situational preference obsolete.

superseded_preferences.fact: Strictly takes prescribed sleep medication at bedtime as her main sleep aid; no longer relies on calming music or pre-bed routines
superseded_preferences.reason: Her treatment for diagnosed sleep apnea now centers on nightly CPAP machine use, which replaces sleep medication as her principal sleep aid unless doctors direct otherwise.

superseded_preferences.fact: Avoids caffeine after 2pm to minimize any interference with prescribed sleep medication and improve chances of restful sleep
superseded_preferences.reason: Her new treatment plan for sleep apnea requires complete elimination of caffeine, not just in the afternoon. This old avoidance schedule is obsolete.
